title: fagus sylvatica f. roseomarginata
intro: Fagus sylvatica f. roseomarginata is a cultivar of the common beech tree, known for its distinctive rose-pink margins on its leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its purported medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Fagus sylvatica f. roseomarginata are thought to include reducing inflammation and improving respiratory health, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of the plant are not commonly used, and it is often grown for ornamental purposes due to its unique foliage, but it can be made into teas or infusions.
